briefing.headline: Vertical and precise — winter muddy conditions call for reaction baits on deep structure.
briefing.gameplan:
In winter with muddy water and pre frontal conditions, reaction presentations shine.

KVD: Blade bait is my #1 bait for covering water in super cold water. Fish get locked on the bottom — short hop it on edges, drops, outside edges of flats and weed lines. Use just heavy enough to get to the zone. Great for largemouth, smallmouth, and spots.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Match the conditions — aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: Uses Lowrance HDS LIVE 12 to find bass in deep wintering holes or around brush piles, then drops a Jigging Rap for vertical presentation; targets bass that are grouped together and competing for food.

KVD: 'Winter bass move shallower than most realize — if you can reach them with crankbait, jerkbait, or slow-rolled spinnerbait in 10ft or less, power baits are first option. Fish avoid expansive flats unless warming trend pushes them to gravel po...

Hackney: 'For winter deep-water jig fishing on lowland reservoirs with stumps and channel swings in 6-8 feet, I use 16-20 pound fluorocarbon. On hard structure like rock I go 14-16 pound. Only downsize to 12 in extremely deep clear water'

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal winter bass stage at 12-25ft on main lake structure. Falling pressure before a front is often the best winter fishing window. Fish move slightly shallower and feed more aggressively than normal winter behavior.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
